info.title: Starlink Enterprise API
info.description:
Description

API to manage Starlink accounts and devices. For interactive endpoints list see: https://starlink.readme.io/

Authentication - OIDC

To authenticate with this API using OIDC, Well Known URL and attach the result to your requests with the Authorize button below.

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

post.description:
Required permission: Service plan, View.
Retrieve the real-time data tracking info for this account's data usage. For detailed instructions, please refer to the API documentation: https://starlink.readme.io/docs/data-usage-api

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Index of page to get.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Amount of service lines to retrieve per page. Defaults to 50, can request up to 250.

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

get.description:
Required permission: Service plan, View.
Gets all products available to this account that could be assigned to service lines. Each product includes data block product info if compatible.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Index of page to get. Page size is 100

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint

get.description:
Required permission: Account information, View.
Returns all addresses on the account in paginated form.

parameters.name: addressIds
parameters.description: Filter by a specific set of Address Reference IDs

parameters.name: metadata
parameters.description: Filter by metadata

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Index of page to get. Page size is 100

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

parameters.name: addressReferenceId
parameters.description: Address reference ID

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

parameters.name: addressReferenceId
parameters.description: Reference Id of address to update

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

get.description:
Required permission: User management, Edit.
Get list of contacts on the authenticated user's account.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Page of contacts to query.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: user_lacks_required_permission

post.description:
Required permission: Admin Only - API User Management, Edit.
Creates the contact if it is new, and adds the contact to the authenticated account.

requestBody.description: Contact information to be added to account.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

200.description: Contact has been created and added to the account with the requested roles.

422.description: Failed to create the contact and add it to the account with the correct roles.

403.description: user_lacks_required_permission

delete.description:
Required permission: Admin Only - API User Management, Edit.
Deletes contact based on specified subject Id on the authenticated account.

parameters.name: subjectId
parameters.description: Subject ID of contact to be deleted.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Forbidden

put.description:
Required permission: Admin Only - API User Management, Edit.
Update contact on the authenticated account.

parameters.name: subjectId
parameters.description: Subject Id of the contact to be updated.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: user_lacks_required_permission

get.summary: Pre-Release: Get all multi-service line enabled data pools.
get.description:
Required permission: Service plan, View.
This endpoint is available for select audiences only.

parameters.name: productReferenceId
parameters.description: Filter by Product ID of the pool

parameters.name: dataPoolIds
parameters.description: Data pool IDs to filter by. Queries for all data pools on account if omitted

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The index of the page, starting at 0. Page size is 100

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Forbidden

get.summary: Pre-Release: Query data pool usage with service line and monthly breakdowns.
get.description:
Required permission: Service plan, View.
This endpoint is available for select audiences only.

parameters.name: dataPoolIds
parameters.description: Data pool Ids to filter by. Queries for usage of all data pools on account if omitted

parameters.name: asOfTimestamp
parameters.description:
Optional timestamp for snapshotting data pool usage *as of* this time. Only includes data blocks
            that are active at this instant (i.e., `StartDate <= asOfTimestamp < EndDate`).
            Defaults to `DateTimeOffset.UtcNow` to return currently active blocks.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The index of the page, starting at 0.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Amount of data pool usage summaries to retrieve per page. Defaults to 50, can request up to 100.

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Forbidden

post.summary: Pre-Release: Enable or disable automatic top-up for a data pool.
post.description:
Required permission: Service plan, Edit.
This endpoint is available for select audiences only.

parameters.name: dataPoolId
parameters.description: The data pool ID

parameters.name: enabled
parameters.description: Whether automatic top-up should be enabled (defaults to true)

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Forbidden

post.description:
Required permission: Aviation flight status management, Edit.
Post a real-time flight event on a Starlink-enabled plane. This endpoint is only accessible from an aviation account.

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Forbidden

post.description: This endpoint is only callable with an authorized provider account.

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Forbidden from creating customer accounts

parameters.name: routerId
parameters.description: Router Id

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

get.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, View.
Return all router configs on this account in paginated form

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Index of page to get. Page size is 100

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

post.description: Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

get.description: Required permission: Device command and configuration, View.

parameters.name: configId
parameters.description: Config Id

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

put.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.
Update a given router config. Any router assigned to this config will immediately receive the update if it is online. Otherwise, the router will receive the update when it comes online.

parameters.name: configId
parameters.description: Config Id

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

put.description:
Required permission: Device configuration assignment, Edit.
Assign the config (or none) to the routers. For each router if it is currently online, the config will immediately be sent to the router. Else, the config will be sent to the router when it comes online. Configs are sent to the router within 1-2 minutes. On error no assignment occurs.

requestBody.description: Request containing config id (or empty) and list of deviceId's to assign to

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

get.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, View.
Gets the router config id that will be assigned to any routers when they are first added to this account.

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to get default router config

put.description:
Required permission: Device configuration assignment, Edit.
Set the default router config on the account. Use an empty string to remove the default config from the account. Any NEW routers on this account will be assigned this config.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to set config as account default

get.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, View.
Gets a paginated list of TLS configs on this account.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Index of page to get. Page size is 100

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

post.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.
Creates a TLS config that allows this certificate and key to be used in router configs when a certificate and key are required by only specifying the certificate (matching key will be added). TLS configs are identified by their certificate string base64 encoded which must be unique on the account.

requestBody.description: Request containing certificate and key, both in base64 encoded pem format

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to create TLS config. Certificates must be unique.

delete.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.
Deletes a TLS certificate and key pair from the set of TLS configs that can be reused when saving router configs and only specifying the certificate. Does not affect any router configs that were already saved, just removes it from list of certificates that the matching key will be inserted on save for.

requestBody.description: Request containing certificate and key, both in base64 encoded pem format

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

post.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.
Upload html file to allow it to be configured as the HTTPS server local content file for router configs. File must be HTML, under 4MB, and file name under 100 characters. Files are stored in a public bucket for configured routers to download. File should be attached as multipart/form-data. See https://starlink.readme.io/docs/local-content for example upload script.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to upload local content file

get.summary: Get list of router local content files
get.description: Required permission: Device command and configuration, View.

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Error getting list of router local content files.

get.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, View.
Returns clients that were unsandboxed through the management API and have access that has not yet expired, paginated 1000 at a time. Clients that are allowed internet access due to sandbox disablement are not returned.

parameters.name: sandboxId
parameters.description: Sandbox Id

parameters.name: expiryAfter
parameters.description: Include clients whose access expires after this time

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Index of page to get. Page size is 1000

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

post.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.
Update the sandbox state for multiple clients. If sandboxing is enabled, sandboxed clients will only have access to domains in the sandbox domain allow list. If a client is duplicated, records the client with the latest expiry.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

put.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.
Heartbeats verify the health of enterprise systems that manage router sandboxing. If heartbeats are not received for an account, Starlink API will instruct routers under the account disable sandboxes until reboot.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

post.description: Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.

parameters.name: routerId
parameters.description: Router Id

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to retrieve the service line

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

parameters.name: reasonForCancellation
parameters.description: Optional reason for cancelling this service line

parameters.name: endNow
parameters.description: If service should end now, or on next bill day. Default is false

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to deactivate the service line

parameters.name: addressReferenceId
parameters.description: Filter by an Address Reference ID

parameters.name: searchString
parameters.description: Filter by fuzzy match of nickname, or exact match on UT ID, UT nickname, UT serial number, or service line number

parameters.name: dataPoolId
parameters.description: Filter for service lines on a given data pool

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The index of the page, starting at 0. Page size is 100

parameters.name: orderByCreatedDateDescending
parameters.description: Sort the paginated results by created date

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Forbidden from retrieving service lines for this account

post.description:
Required permission: Service plan, Edit.
Service line must be linked to an address and a subscription/product-ID, and can optionally be immediately configured with data blocks.

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Forbidden from creating service line on this account

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to update service line nickname

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

requestBody.description: The recurring data blocks to set on the new product, if it on a compatible product. Leave null if otherwise.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to update service line product

put.summary: Set service line public IP setting
put.description:
Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.
Enable or disable public ip setting on a service line

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to set public IP setting

post.description:
Required permission: Service plan, Edit.
Opt in the service line to continue using priority data after reaching the plan capacity. Only applies to some products.

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to opt in service line

post.description:
Required permission: Service plan, Edit.
Opt out the service line from continuing to use priority data even when plan capacity is exceeded. If the service line reaches the plan capacity, it will switch to using standard data. Only applies to some products.

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to opt out service line

post.summary: Add user terminal to service line
post.description:
Required permission: Service plan, Edit.
User terminal must already be on the account. See POST /v2/user-terminals to add user terminal to account.

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

200.description: User terminals added to service line

422.description: Failed to add the user terminal to the service line

delete.summary: Remove user terminal from service line
delete.description:
Required permission: Service plan, Edit.
This removes the user terminal from the service line, but it remains on the account and can be added to another service line. This clears any L2VPN circuits configured for the terminal.

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: User terminal ID, kit serial number, or dish serial number.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

200.description: Removed user terminal from service line

422.description: Failed to remove user terminal from service line

put.summary: Set recurring data blocks on service line
put.description:
Required permission: Service plan, Edit.
Service line must be on a top up plan

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

200.description: Successfully set recurring blocks on service line

422.description: Failed to set service line recurring data blocks

post.summary: Adds one-time top up data block to service line
post.description:
Required permission: Service plan, Edit.
Service line must be on a top up plan

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: The service line number

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to update add data block to service line

get.description:
Required permission: Service plan, View.
Get the previous billing partial periods for this service line. For more information about this endpoint, see https://starlink.readme.io/docs/understanding-proration.

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

patch.summary: Pre-Release: Set whether a service line should consume from a data pool.
patch.description:
Required permission: Service plan, Edit.
This endpoint is available for select audiences only.

parameters.name: serviceLineNumber
parameters.description: Service line number

parameters.name: consumeFromPool
parameters.description: Whether the service line should consume from pool, defaults to true

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

get.description:
Required permission: Device management, View.
Gets all user terminals on the account, filtered by the optional filter parameters, in paginated form

parameters.name: serviceLineNumbers
parameters.description: Filter by a set of service line numbers

parameters.name: userTerminalIds
parameters.description: Filter by a set of user terminal IDs

parameters.name: hasServiceLine
parameters.description: Filter by user terminals with or without a services lines. Omitting this will return both sets

parameters.name: searchString
parameters.description: Filter by partial match of user terminal ID, serial number, or kit serial number

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The index of the page, starting at 0. Page size is 100

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

post.description:
Required permission: Device management, Edit.
This will add the user terminal to the account, but won't start service. User terminals must be present on the account before adding to a service line.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

422.description: Failed to add user terminal to the account

delete.description:
Required permission: Device management, Edit.
User terminal must already be removed from service lines before it can be removed from the account. See /v2/service-lines/{serviceLineNumber}/user-terminals/{deviceId}

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: User terminal ID, kit serial number, or dish serial number.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

200.description: Removed user terminal from service line

422.description: Failed to remove user terminal from account

post.description: Required permission: Device command and configuration, Edit.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: User terminal ID, kit serial number, or dish serial number.

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

put.description:
Required permission: Device configuration assignment, Edit.
Assign the config (or none) to the user terminals. For each terminal if it is currently online, the config will immediately be sent. Else, the config will be sent when it comes online. On error no assignment occurs. Currently terminal configIds are only visible on starlink website.

requestBody.description: Request containing config id (or empty) and list of userTerminalIds to assign to

400.description: Bad request. Invalid or missing parameter

403.description: Forbidden, either you don't have access to the account or a terminal or config is not on the account.

get.summary: Get L2VPN circuits available for this account
get.description: Required permission: Device command and configuration, View.

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

put.summary: Set L2VPN VLANs for user terminal
put.description:
Required permission: Device configuration assignment, Edit.
Terminal must be on subscription that allows L2VPN. Maximum 2 circuits per Starlink PoP. Use null or empty circuit list to clear all L2VPN circuits for terminal. See /user-terminals/available-circuits for all configurable circuits and associated Starlink PoP.

parameters.name: deviceId
parameters.description: User terminal ID, kit serial number, or dish serial number.

requestBody.description: List of circuits and associated VLAN tags to assign to user terminal. Terminal must be on a service line with subscription that allows L2VPN.

400.description: Bad Request

403.description: Missing required permission for this endpoint or resource

regionCode.description: The region code of the account. Example: US

count.description: Quantity of the specified data block to add

fileContentId.description: File id to go in router config HTTPS server FileContentId field

fileContentHash.description: File MD5 hash to go in router config HTTPS server FileContentHash field

addressLines.description: The address lines for the address. Example: 1 Rocket Road

locality.description: The town/locality of the address. Example: Hawthorne

administrativeArea.description: The administrative area of the address. Example: California

administrativeAreaCode.description: The administrative area code of the address. Example: CA

region.description: The region of the address. Example: United States

regionCode.description: The region code of the address. Example: US

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address. Example: 90250-6844

metadata.description: Optional field that can be used to store information about the address for external purposes

formattedAddress.description: A user readable address. Example: 1 Rocket Road, Hawthorne, CA 90250-6844, US

latitude.description: The required latitude of the address. Example: 33.92

longitude.description: The required longitude of the address. Example: -118.32

locality.description: The town/locality of the address. Example: Hawthorne

administrativeArea.description: The administrative area of the address. Example: California

administrativeAreaCode.description: The administrative area code of the address. Example: CA

region.description: The region of the address. Example: United States

regionCode.description: The region code of the address. Example: US

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address. Example: 90250

metadata.description: Optional field that can be used to store information about the address for external purposes

formattedAddress.description: A user readable address. Example: 1 Rocket Road, Hawthorne, CA 90250-6844, US

latitude.description: The latitude of the address: Example 33.92

longitude.description: The longitude of the address. Example: -118.32

addressLines.description: The address lines for the address. Example: 1 Rocket Road

locality.description: The town/locality of the address. Example: Hawthorne

administrativeArea.description: The administrative area of the address. Example: California

administrativeAreaCode.description: The administrative area code of the address. Example: CA

region.description: The region of the address. Example: United States

regionCode.description: The region code of the address. Example: US

postalCode.description: The postal code of the address. Example: 90250-6844

metadata.description: Optional field that can be used to store information about the address for external purposes

formattedAddress.description: A user readable address. Example: 1 Rocket Road, Hawthorne, CA 90250-6844, US

latitude.description: The required latitude of the address. Example: 33.92

longitude.description: The required longitude of the address. Example: -118.32

airportIataCode.description: 3 character string representing airport code in IATA format.

airportIcaoCode.description: 4 character string representing airport code in ICAO format.

configId.description: Config id (or none) to assign to routers
Config id (or none) to assign to user terminals

businessName.description: Business name only required for enterprise customers.

startDate.description: Start date of the respective month.

endDate.description: End date of the respective month.

serviceLineUsage.description: Montly usage breakdown by service lines.

startDate.description: Start date of the respective month.

endDate.description: End date of the respective month.

serviceLineUsage.description: Montly usage breakdown by service lines.

productId.description: Product ID of the data block.

startDate.description: Start date of the data block, i.e. when it is active for consumption.

endDate.description: End date of the data block, i.e. when it is no longer active for consumption.

isPrepaid.description: If the data block is prepaid.

consumedAmountGB.description: GB amount that the service line consumed from the block.
GB amount that the service line consumed from the block.

count.description: How many blocks of this type there are.

dataAmount.description: The data amount of each block.

dataUnitType.description: The unit that the data amount is in.

startDateUtc.description: UTC start date of the data block.

expirationDateUtc.description: UTC expiration date of the data block.

totalAmountGB.description: Total GB capacity, calculated by multiplying BlocksCount and PerBlockAmountGB.

consumedAmountGB.description: How much data has been used on the block.

productId.description: Product ID of the data block.

totalPrice.description: Total price, calculated by multiplying PerBlockPrice and BlocksCount.

startDate.description: UTC start date of the data block.

endDate.description: UTC end date of the data block.

totalAmountGB.description: Total GB capacity, calculated by multiplying BlocksCount and PerBlockAmountGB.

consumedAmountGB.description: How much data has been used on the block.

productId.description: Product ID of the data block.

automaticTopUpEnabled.description: If the data pool is opted-in to overage top-ups.

restricted.description: If the data pool and service lines on it are restricted due to exhausting their data

dataBlocks.description: Active data blocks on the data pool.

productId.description: Product ID of the data pool.

accountNumber.description: Account number that owns this data pool.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when the data pool usage was last updated (UTC).

dataBlocks.description: Usage by data blocks in the pool.
Usage by data blocks in the pool.

dataBlockProducts.description: The data block products that are available for this service plan.

isoCurrencyCode.description: ISO currency code for displaying prices (e.g., "USD", "EUR"). Null if no subscription exists.

isMobilePlan.description: Indicates if this is a mobile-type plan (mobility class is nomadic or mobile).

activeFrom.description: Date when data usage tracking began for this product (UTC). May differ from subscription start date.

subscriptionActiveFrom.description: Date when the service line's subscription started (UTC).

subscriptionEndDate.description: Date when the subscription ended (UTC). Null for active subscriptions.

overageName.description: Display name for the overage product shown in opt-in/out interfaces. Empty string if no overage product exists.

overageDescription.description: Description of the overage product shown in opt-in/out interfaces. Empty string if no overage product exists.

isOptedIntoOverage.description: Indicates whether the service line is currently opted into overage for metered plans.

overageLineDeactivatedDate.description: Most recent date and time when overage opt-in was disabled (UTC). Null if never disabled or currently opted in.

productId.description: Product ID identifying the current subscription plan. Empty string if no subscription exists.

usageLimitGB.description: Usage limit in GB for this plan. For metered plans, this is the priority data limit. For Priority data plans, this is the total data pool capacity.

dataCategoryMapping.description: Deprecated. Value will always be empty dictionary.

startDate.description: Start date of the billing cycle.

endDate.description: End date of the billing cycle.

dailyDataUsage.description: Daily data usage breakdown for each day in the billing cycle. Omits days with no recorded usage.

overageLines.description: Overage lines for metered plans showing usage beyond plan limits. Empty for plans without overage or cycles with no overage. May contain multiple lines if subscription changed mid-cycle.

dataPoolUsage.description: Data pool usage information for Priority data plans. Empty for metered plans or if no data pools are attached.

totalPriorityGB.description: Total priority data consumed in this billing cycle (GB), rounded to 2 decimal places. Includes opt-in priority data.

totalStandardGB.description: Total standard data consumed in this billing cycle (GB), rounded to 2 decimal places.

totalOptInPriorityGB.description: Total priority data consumed while opted into overage in this billing cycle (GB), rounded to 2 decimal places. This is a subset of TotalPriorityGB.

totalNonBillableGB.description: Total non-billable data consumed in this billing cycle (GB), rounded to 2 decimal places.

priorityGB.description: Total priority (unrestricted, high-speed) data consumed on this day in GB. Includes opt-in priority data.

optInPriorityGB.description: Portion of priority data consumed while opted into overage on this day in GB. This is a subset of PriorityGB, not additive.

standardGB.description: Standard (deprioritized) data consumed on this day in GB.

nonBillableGB.description: Non-billable data consumed on this day in GB.

pricePerGB.description: Price per GB for overage consumption (e.g., 1.00 for $1.00/GB).

usageLimitGB.description: Plan's included priority data limit in GB before overage charges apply. Zero for plans that are restricted by default.

overageAmountGB.description: Amount of priority data consumed beyond the usage limit while opted into overage, in GB. Only opt-in priority data counts toward overage.

consumedAmountGB.description: Amount of priority data consumed up to the usage limit, in GB.

overagePrice.description: Total overage cost before taxes and fees.

productId.description: Product ID for the overage product.

activeFrom.description: Date when this overage period began tracking usage (UTC). Null if no usage has been recorded yet.

configId.description: Router configId that will be assigned to routers when they are first added to this account. Does not affect routers that are assigned no config.

certificateBase64Pem.description: Certificate base64 encoded pem string to match on for deletion of the corresponding certificate and key pair

deviceId.description: User terminal Id, kit serial number, or dish serial number. Ex: 12345678-12345678-12345678

circuitId.description: Starlink generated Id of L2VPN circuit.

customerVlans.description: Customer VLANs are the inner tag and identify L2VPN customer traffic to and from a user terminal.

serviceVlan.description: Outer VLAN tag used for Q-in-Q and identifies a single ethernet virtual circuit (EVC) for a user terminal. Null if no service VLAN configured.

circuitId.description: Starlink generated Id of L2VPN circuit.

popName.description: Starlink PoP associated with this circuit.

circuitId.description: Starlink generated Id of L2VPN circuit.

customerVlans.description: Customer VLANs are the inner tag and identify L2VPN customer traffic to and from a user terminal.

serviceVlan.description: Outer VLAN tag used for Q-in-Q and identifies a single ethernet virtual circuit (EVC) for a user terminal. Null or 0 if no service VLAN tag should be added.

isInOptInCoolDown.description: Whether service line is in cool down period from recently changing opted in state

periodStart.description: The timestamp of the start of the partial period

periodEnd.description: The timestamp of the end of the partial period

serviceLineNumbers.description: Service line numbers to filter by. Queries for all service lines on account if omitted

previousBillingCycles.description: Number of previous billing cycles to include in addition to the current one. Defaults to 1

activeServiceLinesOnly.description: Whether to filter by only active service lines. Defaults to true

queryStartDate.description: Override the first cycle's start date with the provided query start date

dataPoolId.description: If provided, onboard the service line onto an existing data pool. The data pool must be multi-service-line enabled. This parameter is mutually exclusive with RecurringDataBlocks.

applyToCurrentMonth.description: When true, creates additional blocks for the current billing cycle so the current month data block counts match the new recurring configuration.

nickname.description: Nickname for this config. Must be less than 100 characters.

fileContentId.description: Id of uploaded local content file, used in router config HTTPS server

fileContentHash.description: MD5 hash of the uploaded file used to verify integrity when router downloads the file for hosting. Used in router config HTTPS server

userTerminalId.description: User terminal Id this router is bonded to.

configId.description: Router config this router is assigned to, or null if no config assigned.

hardwareVersion.description: Deprecated field. Value will always be null

lastBonded.description: Deprecated field. Value will always be null

healthy.description: True if client systems are healthy, false otherwise.

addressReferenceId.description: Address Reference ID to associate with the service line. Example: 55ec6574-10d8-bd9c-1951-d4184f4ae467

productReferenceId.description: Subscription Product ID to associate with the service line. Example: business-subscription-100

dataPoolId.description: Pre-Release: The ID of an existing multi-service-line data pool to which a new service line should be added. This field is mutually exclusive with RecurringDataBlocks and cannot be used together with it. Available for select audiences only.

recurringBlocksCurrentBillingCycle.description: The current billing cycle's monthly recurring data blocks.

recurringBlocksNextBillingCycle.description: Next billing cycle's monthly recurring data blocks.

delayedProductRecurringBlocksNextCycle.description: What recurring blocks will be when the service line switches to the delayed product.

topUpBlocksOptInPurchase.description: What top up blocks were automatically purchased because the service line was out of data and opted in.

topUpBlocksOneTimePurchase.description: What top up blocks were manually purchased.

accountNumber.description: Account number that owns this service line.

serviceLineNumber.description: Service line number for which usage data is reported.

startDate.description: Start date of the first billing cycle in the response (UTC).

endDate.description: End date of the last billing cycle in the response (UTC).

billingCycles.description: Data usage grouped by billing cycle, ordered chronologically. The most recent cycle appears last in the array.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when the usage data was last updated in the cache. Only present for cached data (typically yesterday and later).

addressReferenceId.description: Address Reference ID of the address associated with the service line. Example: 55ec6574-10d8-bd9c-1951-d4184f4ae467

serviceLineNumber.description: The Service Line Number. Example: AST-511274-31364-54

nickname.description: A user-defined nickname for this service line.

delayedProductId.description: Scheduled product change for next bill date.

optInProductId.description: Opt-in product id, opted out if empty.

startDate.description: The start date of the subscription. This is in UTC.

endDate.description: The service line deactivation date, which only appears if the service line is deactivated. This is in UTC.

publicIp.description: Indicates if service line is public IP.

active.description: Indicates if service line is active.

publicIp.description: If public IP should be enabled or not

nickname.description: New nickname for the existing service line.

tailNumber.description: Tail Number that matches aircraft registration.

flightNumber.description: Flight Number that specifies specific route operated by airlines. Please include 2-character airline code in IATA format followed by 1-4 digits.

eventTimeUtc.description: ISO 8601 Timestamp that represents actual gate departure (if eventType = Departure) or gate arrival (if eventType = Arrival) time in UTC.

estimatedGateArrivalTimeUtc.description: Optional Metadata. ISO 8601 Timestamp that represents estimated gate arrival time in UTC.

passengerCount.description: Optional Metadata. Actual passenger count on flight.

slaOptOutFlight.description: Optional Metadata. Exclude this flight from Starlink SLA commitments. Default is false.

maxNumberOfUserTerminals.description: The maximum number of user terminals that can be assigned to a single service line using this product. Null response means an unlimited number of UTs can be assigned.

certificateBase64Pem.description: TLS certificate in PEM format, base64 encoded.

keyBase64Pem.description: TLS key in PEM format, base64 encoded. Must match the public key of the certificate.

clientId.description: Identifier of the client to add or remove from a sandbox.

sandboxId.description: Identifier used to separate sandbox environments. Per-client sandboxing is shared within the same SandboxId.

expiry.description: Expiry of the sandbox state as a UTC date time.

configId.description: ConfigId or none to set as account default config. Use empty string to clear default config setting.

dataPoolId.description: Pre-Release: If provided, onboard the service line onto an existing multi-service-line data pool. This parameter is mutually exclusive with RecurringDataBlocks. Available for select audiences only.

delayUpdate.description: Specifies when the change will be applied. True forces changes to be scheduled on the next billing cycle, False forces changes to occur immediately, Null uses legacy logic allowing upgrades to occur immediately and downgrades to be delayed.

subjectId.description: Unique subject ID given to this user.

roles.description: List of User roles which map to permissions on the specified account.

userTerminalId.description: User Terminal ID. This ID is not printed anywhere on the kit or hardware. Example: 00020900-002220cc-225b9199

kitSerialNumber.description: Kit Serial Number. This ID can be found on the box that the hardware came in. Example: KIT00142069

dishSerialNumber.description: Dish Serial Number. This ID can be found on the dish itself. Example: 2DHT00542069

serviceLineNumber.description: The service line the user terminal is associated with if it has service, Example: AST-511274-31364-54

l2VpnCircuits.description: List of L2VPN circuits and associated VLANs configured for this terminal.

routers.description: Routers currently bonded to this UT.
